featuretablefilter 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • featuretablefilter Tutorial: Complete Guide to Microbiome Feature Table Filtering

    Compute quality control metrics comparing original and filtered tables

    compute_filtering_qc.Rd

    Calculates multiple QC metrics to assess the impact of filtering on a feature table. Includes sparsity change, retention rates, rank-abundance stability, compositional similarity, and diversity retention (Effective Number of Species / Hill numbers).

    compute_filtering_qc(original_table, filtered_table, top_n = 10)

    Arguments

    original_table

    Original feature table (data.frame) before filtering.

    filtered_table

    Filtered feature table (data.frame) after filtering.

    top_n

    Number of top taxa to consider for Rank-Abundance Stability. Default is 10.

    Value

    A list containing:

    sparsity_original

    Sparsity of original table

    sparsity_filtered

    Sparsity of filtered table

    sparsity_drop_percent

    Percentage point drop in sparsity

    read_retention_percent

    Percentage of reads retained

    feature_retention_percent

    Percentage of features retained

    sample_retention_percent

    Percentage of samples retained

    rank_abundance_correlation

    Spearman correlation of top N taxa abundances

    rank_abundance_pvalue

    P-value for rank-abundance correlation

    procrustes_m2

    Procrustes M^2 statistic (Bray-Curtis based)

    procrustes_correlation

    Procrustes correlation coefficient

    shannon_ens_original

    Mean Shannon effective number of species (Hill q=1) across samples

    shannon_ens_filtered

    Mean Shannon ENS after filtering

    shannon_ens_retention_percent

    Percentage of Shannon ENS retained

    simpson_ens_original

    Mean Simpson effective number of species (Hill q=2) across samples

    simpson_ens_filtered

    Mean Simpson ENS after filtering

    simpson_ens_retention_percent

    Percentage of Simpson ENS retained

    Examples

    # Compare original and filtered tables
# qc <- compute_filtering_qc(original_table, filtered_table)
# print(qc)

# Access diversity retention metrics
# qc$shannon_ens_retention_percent  # How much Shannon diversity (q=1) was retained
# qc$simpson_ens_retention_percent  # How much Simpson diversity (q=2) was retained

    Contents
